notes: MB search match (sim 1.0) confirms Lage Lund, 2022, and all five keyTracks appear in the MB tracklist. Label 'Unknown' is honest (MB has no label). embedded_mbid confirms Duke Ellington, label Legacy, release 2023-09-15. This is a 2023 chronological compilation of Ellington's 1932 recordings (alternate takes throughout). All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist. albumDNA says 'Recorded in 2023 for Legacy' which is imprecise — the music was recorded in 1932; 2023 is the compilation's release date. MB has no recording-date field to quote as a contradiction, so this is noted, not flagged as factual_error per the absence-is-not-contradiction rule.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: a collaborative album by Cuban pianist Gonzalo Rubalcaba and guitarist Jorge Pedrón
wrongClaims.problem: The collaborator's name and instrument are wrong. The album is a duo with Pierrick Pedron (an alto saxophonist), not a guitarist named 'Jorge Pedron'. The name 'Jorge Pedron' is fabricated.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: Pedrón's guitar work in an intimate chamber setting
wrongClaims.problem: Attributes guitar to the collaborator; the credited partner is Pierrick Pedron, listed in our entity's own discography simply as the Pedron Rubalcaba co-leader.

notes: Right album (embedded_mbid: title 'Pédron Rubalcaba', GAZEBO GAZ 217, 2023-06-09; Gonzalo Rubalcaba is a credited artist and 'Cuban pianist' matches the stored Rubalcaba Wikipedia page). All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist. The error is the collaborator: MB credits 'Pierrick Pédron' and the Rubalcaba discography lists 'Pédron Rubalcaba with Pierrick Pédron', but ours invents 'guitarist Jorge Pedrón'. Wrong first name and wrong instrument. Evidence does not state Pierrick Pedron's instrument explicitly, so the 'guitarist' descriptor is technically unverifiable from the pack, but the NAME contradiction alone is a clear factual_error. wikiLink (Gonzalo Rubalcaba page) is about our entity = correct.

embedded_mbid confirms Jason Moran (with Marcus Gilmore & BlankFor.ms), Red Hook Records, 2023-09-01. All five keyTracks (Onset I/II, Tape Loop A, etc.) match the MB tracklist. 'pianist Jason Moran' agrees with the stored Jason Moran (musician) Wikipedia page (our entity). wikiLink correct.

embedded_mbid confirms 'Trio Tapestry' by Joe Lovano, ECM. All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist exactly, and the personnel named (Crispell piano, Castaldi percussion) align with the same trio seen in the our-daily-bread release. albumDNA narrates the original album history: 'recorded in March 2018 and released on ECM January the following year' (i.e. Jan 2019), 'his debut as bandleader for the label'. The MB release here is dated 2023 (releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 2023, and our year field is 2023), which does not match a Jan-2019 release — likely this MBID is a later pressing/reissue. There is no quotable evidence passage contradicting the 2018/2019 recording-and-release narrative, so per absence-is-not-contradiction this is left clean with a flag: the 2018/2019 dates are unverifiable against this pack and sit oddly next to the 2023 release date. 'man percussionist' is a garble for 'and percussionist'.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: It was released on May 3, 2024, through Young.

notes: Right album: embedded_mbid and the stored 'Fearless Movement (album)' Wikipedia page both confirm Kamasi Washington, 2024-05-03, and the albumDNA is lifted verbatim from that wiki page (correct entity, fifth studio album, West Coast Get Down personnel, etc.). All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist. The structured label field is wrong: ours says 'Shoto Mas Inc.' while MB authoritative label is 'Young' and the wiki says 'released... through Young'. (The albumDNA text itself correctly says 'through Young'; only the label field disagrees.) Flagged factual_error on the label field.

Right album: embedded_mbid and the stored 'MoonDial' Wikipedia page both confirm Pat Metheny, 2024-07-26, baritone-guitar solo album; albumDNA is verbatim from that wiki page (correct entity). All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist. Label-field discrepancy: ours 'BMG' vs MB authoritative 'Modern Recordings'. Modern Recordings is in practice a BMG imprint, so 'BMG' is the parent rather than a wrong entity (comparable to the spec's CBS/Columbia example), but recorded as a fieldIssue/factual_error since the authoritative string differs. Low confidence because this is a parent/imprint relationship, not a true contradiction; a repair writer may prefer to keep 'Modern Recordings'.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: "Pink Elephants on Parade" is a song and scene from the 1941 Disney animated feature film Dumbo in which Dumbo and Timothy Q. Mouse, having accidentally become intoxicated, see pink elephants sing, dance, and play musical instruments during a hallucination sequence.
wrongClaims.problem: The entire albumDNA describes the Disney 'Dumbo' film song/scene, a different kind of thing, not the Sun Ra Arkestra album. Classic disambiguation-collision: the prose was scraped from the Wikipedia article about the Disney song.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Pink Elephants on Parade — Sun Ra, Arkestra — tracks: The Forest of No Return; Someday My Prince Will Come; Zip-A-Dee-Doo-Dah; Let's Go Fly a Kite; Second Star to the Right; Pink Elephants on Parade...

notes: The album fields are correct for the Sun Ra Arkestra release (embedded_mbid: 'Pink Elephants on Parade', Sun Ra/Arkestra, 2024-07-05 — a live-recordings collection of Disney covers), and all six keyTracks (with venue suffixes like '(9:30 Club)', '(Regatta Bar 1990)') match the MB tracklist ignoring suffixes, so keyTracksVerdict is ok. But the albumDNA prose is entirely about the 1941 Dumbo film song/scene — wrong entity content (a film sequence, not the album). Verdict entity_mismatch reflects the hijacked prose; entityMatch.musicbrainz is 'yes' only because the MB release genuinely is our album. Repair writer must rewrite albumDNA to describe the Sun Ra Arkestra album.

Right album: embedded_mbid confirms 'Remembrance' by Chick Corea & Béla Fleck, 2024-05-10. All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist. The albumDNA is a generic Chick Corea biography lifted verbatim from the stored 'Chick Corea discography' Wikipedia page (about our artist — born 1941 Chelsea, Tones for Joan's Bones 1966, played with Miles Davis/Stan Getz, composed Spain/La Fiesta/Windows); every bio fact checks out against that extract, and it is a legitimate (if album-irrelevant) artist bio, NOT wrong-entity content. The bio text is also truncated mid-word ('La Fiesta', and 'Wi.'). Only issue: label field ours 'Thirty Tigers' vs MB authoritative 'Béla Fleck Productions Inc.' (Thirty Tigers is plausibly the distributor, not the label). Recorded as a label fieldIssue/factual_error; confidence moderate given the distributor/label ambiguity. wikiLink is the Chick Corea discography page = about our entity = correct.
